extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, whose condition is (elderly AND hepatic_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency AND anticoagulated AND polypharmacy AND diabetes). rec_drug_b is set true by r3 whenever elderly is true. Since r4's condition includes elderly as a conjunct, every input that fires r4 also fires r3. No rule ever sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false (defaults only apply when an output is left unset, and priorities only choose among true-setting rules), so there is no override path. Therefore setting all six of r4's conjuncts true makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The counterexample sets elderly, hepatic_impairment, g6pd_deficiency, anticoagulated, polypharmacy, diabetes = true; immunosuppressed and hyperkalemia = false (they only affect rec_drug_c). This yields rec_drug_a = true (r4) and rec_drug_b = true (r3).
